hook.text: Some magma flows like honey. Others are so thick they explode. The silica content decides everything.

buildup.text: Magma types include basaltic (low silica, fluid), andesitic (medium), and rhyolitic (high silica, thick).

discovery.text: Basaltic magma erupts gently at shield volcanoes. Rhyolitic magma traps gas and erupts explosively.

twist.text: Silica acts like a thickener. More silica means higher viscosity, more trapped gas, and more violent eruptions.

climax.text: Knowing the magma type lets geologists predict eruption style. Silica content is a volcano's personality test.

punchline.text: Silica decides if a volcano whispers or screams.

quiz.question: Which magma type is most explosive?
